html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

elements: Five idealized pinhole cameras (each with a rectangular image plane, rays converging to a pinhole, and horizontal orientation)
Thick horizontal red arrows below each camera pointing to the right indicating lateral translation direction
Thin lines connecting each pinhole to the image plane to illustrate geometry

interactions.label: Lateral speed
interactions.teaches: Shows how changing the camera's lateral translation speed affects the sense of observed motion in the image plane.

interactions.label: Number of cameras
interactions.teaches: Lets you compare the trajectory by varying the number of camera positions shown along the lateral path.

demo_steps.title: Static camera reference
demo_steps.narration: Let's start with a single camera at rest. Notice that with no lateral speed, there is no visible shift or parallax—every camera is in the same place.

demo_steps.title: Visualizing lateral motion
demo_steps.narration: Now, let's move the camera laterally at a moderate speed and show five positions. The red arrows illustrate the direction and magnitude of the camera's movement. This movement is what you would experience looking sideways out of a moving car.

demo_steps.title: Increasing speed exaggerates motion
demo_steps.narration: Increasing the lateral speed (while keeping the number of cameras constant) stretches the spacing between each camera, showing faster translation. This amplifies the parallax effect and makes nearby objects in the scene appear to move faster across your view.

demo_steps.title: More positions, smoother trajectory
demo_steps.narration: Let’s increase the number of camera snapshots along the path. More cameras give a clearer sense of the continuous lateral motion and the geometry of the translation.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Isometric view from slightly above and to the front, so the row of cameras and the direction of motion (red arrows) are clearly visible. Zoomed to frame the full length of the camera array.
interactionPlan.notes: Use bold, semi-transparent arrows for the direction of motion. Make cameras identical and evenly spaced along the X-axis. Render the camera frusta as simple line geometry for clarity.

attempts.html:
Visualizing lateral motion
Move the sliders to vary the lateral camera speed and number of shown camera positions.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Camera icons in the generated figure are angled and have perspective, but the original figure's cameras are flat 2D wireframes with no tilt or perspective.
The red arrows in the generated figure are short and floated below each camera, while the original figure uses long arrows extending horizontally with heads much larger than the shafts.
The generated figure shows only 5 cameras at the default, but the reference image contains 6 cameras.
Camera spacing and alignment are not perfectly horizontal as in the reference (the generated arrangement is slightly diagonal due to camera angle).
There are interactive UI labels and controls present in the generated figure that are not present in the source.
evaluation.notes: Useful interactivity and UI, but perspective, arrow design, count, and camera arrangement do not faithfully match the flat, evenly spaced textbook figure.
action_items: Orient all camera drawings fully upright and parallel to the viewing plane for a flat, textbook-like 2D appearance with no perspective.
Increase red arrow length and make the arrowheads much larger relative to the shaft, positioning them directly in line with the cameras and fully horizontal.
Correct the default number of cameras to 6 to match the original and ensure even spacing without diagonal offset.
Remove or hide UI controls for figure renders intended to match the source figure exactly.
Ensure all camera icons are outlined and styled exactly as in the original blueprint-style figure, without 3D shading.

feedback.rationale: The concept and elements chosen are correct, but the implementation details such as camera styling, arrow design, number of cameras, and excess UI controls deviate from the source and should be fixed in the generation.
action_items: Orient all camera drawings fully upright and parallel to the viewing plane for a flat, textbook-like 2D appearance with no perspective.
Increase red arrow length and make the arrowheads much larger relative to the shaft, positioning them directly in line with the cameras and fully horizontal.
Correct the default number of cameras to 6 to match the original and ensure even spacing without diagonal offset.
Remove or hide UI controls for figure renders intended to match the source figure exactly.
Ensure all camera icons are outlined and styled exactly as in the original blueprint-style figure, without 3D shading.

attempts.html:
Visualizing lateral motion
Five idealized pinhole cameras translate laterally; the red arrows indicate motion to the right.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Extra UI elements (sliders, buttons, narration) not present in the original figure
Arrows are slightly smaller and closer to the cameras than in the source figure
First and last arrows in the generated figure do not extend as far outward as those in the original
Camera geometries, while close, are not as thickly outlined as in the textbook image
evaluation.notes: Cameras and motion arrows are visually and conceptually correct, but extra UI and minor visual differences affect faithfulness.
action_items: Adjust arrow size and placement so they extend further past the outer cameras, matching the original's composition.
Thicken the lines for the camera outlines to enhance visual correspondence with the textbook figure.
Provide an option to hide all explanatory UI for a pure diagram view resembling the original.

feedback.rationale: All issues pertain to UI extras and minor visual/style adjustments, indicating the plan is correct and only the figure's execution needs refinement.
action_items: Adjust arrow size and placement so they extend further past the outer cameras, matching the original's composition.
Thicken the lines for the camera outlines to enhance visual correspondence with the textbook figure.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: There are only 5 cameras/arrows in the screenshot, but the source image has 6 cameras and 6 arrows.
The arrows in the generated figure are shorter and less closely spaced than in the source image.
Camera icons in the generated figure are slightly boxier and not as wide/flat as in the source.
Arrow shapes in the generated figure are thicker and less streamlined compared to the original.
Extra UI controls and step buttons are present which are not in the original figure.
evaluation.notes: Interactivity is strong and the general idea is recognizable, but camera and arrow count/spacing and proportions do not match the original.
action_items: Increase the number of default cameras/arrows from 5 to 6 to match the reference.
Adjust the arrow length, shape, and spacing to more closely replicate the thin, long arrows in the source figure.
Refine the camera icon geometry to be wider and flatter, better imitating the original style.
Remove or hide interactive controls in static screenshot mode for visual faithfulness.
Reduce default scale and spacing so the whole camera/arrow chain fits more proportionally as in the source image.

feedback.rationale: The action items are focused on adjusting quantity, proportions, geometry, and UI details, indicating the plan is conceptually sound but the implementation needs executional refinement.

Refine the camera icon geometry to be wider and flatter, better imitating the original style.

Reduce default scale and spacing so the whole camera/arrow chain fits more proportionally as in the source image.
